message:
feat(fnp): port 6 comparison + 4 logical ufuncs to native Rust

Continue the NumPy-passthrough → native-Rust port pattern (heaviside,
maximum/minimum, fmax/fmin, arctan2, gcd/lcm, fmod/remainder/
float_power) onto the comparison and logical ufunc surfaces. Ten
ufuncs land here in one commit:

Comparison ufuncs (return DType::Bool, length-matched broadcast):
  - equal           (x1 == x2)
  - not_equal       (x1 != x2)
  - greater         (x1 >  x2)
  - greater_equal   (x1 >= x2)
  - less            (x1 <  x2)
  - less_equal      (x1 <= x2)

Logical ufuncs (treat operands as boolean: nonzero → True):
  - logical_and     (binary)
  - logical_or      (binary)
  - logical_xor     (binary)
  - logical_not     (unary)

fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs (10 new pub fn, ~200 LOC)
----------------------------------------------
Each comparison ufunc broadcasts via UFuncArray::broadcast_arrays
and emits a DType::Bool result with values 1.0 / 0.0 in the same
shape. Logical ops treat any nonzero finite as True (matching
NumPy's truthiness rules including +0.0 → False, -0.0 → False,
NaN → True). integer_sidecar is preserved as None on the result —
boolean ufuncs always lose the integer sidecar.

fnp-python/src/lib.rs (10 new dispatcher wrappers + 10 #[pyfunction]
repoints, ~150 LOC)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Each wrapper follows the established pattern:

  if no kwargs && args.len() == 2 (or 1 for logical_not):
      extract_numeric_array(...)
      ufunc_<name>(...)
      build_numpy_array_from_ufunc(...)
  else:
      core_numpy_passthrough(...)   // preserves out=, where=, etc.

The existing #[pyfunction] entry points for `equal`, `not_equal`,
`greater`, `greater_equal`, `less`, `less_equal`, `logical_and`,
`logical_or`, `logical_xor`, `logical_not` are repointed from
core_numpy_passthrough to the matching native_binary_*_or_passthrough
/ native_unary_logical_not_or_passthrough wrappers.

The native path covers the hot 2-arg / 1-arg case; any caller
passing `out=`, `where=`, `casting=`, or other ufunc kwargs falls
through to NumPy unchanged.

This brings the comparison + logical ufunc surface to full native
parity with NumPy for the common case, removing 10 hot
NumPy-roundtrips per call site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
